meaning: n.板条,木条(铺室内地面的) 船舱周框的压条
examples: Capitalists batten on the surplus value created by workers.
Another expression linked to sailing is batten down the hatches .
On closer inspection, however, it becomes clear that the crisis forced the industry as a whole to batten down the hatches.
However UKIP MEP Gerard Batten said people wanted "maximum standards not minimum standards" on mutual recognition.
Dayne Batten is affiliated with the University of North Carolina Department of Public Policy.
